lessons.title: Perimeter and Area of Plane Shapes

content.body:
Rectangle: P=2(l+w), A=lw
Square: P=4s, A=s²
Triangle: A=½bh, P=a+b+c
Circle: C=2πr=πd, A=πr²
Parallelogram: A=bh
Trapezium: A=½(a+b)h

content.title: Area Calculations
content.body:
Rectangle 8cm × 5cm: A=40cm²
Circle r=7cm: A=π×49≈154cm²
Triangle base=10, height=6: A=½×10×6=30cm²
Trapezium parallel sides 4 and 8, height 5: A=½(4+8)×5=30cm²

content.title: Compound Shapes
content.body:
Break into simpler shapes, find each area, add or subtract.
L-shaped: split into two rectangles
Annulus (ring): A=π(R²-r²)

questions.question: Area of trapezium with parallel sides 6 and 10, height 4
An annulus has outer radius 10 and inner radius 6. Area (π=3.14)
What does simplifying an expression mean?
options: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.answer: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.explanation: Simplification means rewriting an expression in its simplest equivalent form by collecting like terms and applying arithmetic.

questions.question: Which of these is an example of a variable?
questions.explanation: A variable is a letter (like x, y, n) that represents an unknown or changing value.

questions.question: What is 2x + 3x simplified?
questions.explanation: Collect like terms: 2x + 3x = 5x (both terms have the same variable x).

questions.question: Solve for x: x + 7 = 15
questions.explanation: Subtract 7 from both sides: x = 15 - 7 = 8.

questions.question: What is the result of expanding 4(y - 3)?
questions.explanation: Distribute: 4 × y - 4 × 3 = 4y - 12.

questions.question: Which operation reverses addition?
questions.explanation: Subtraction is the inverse (reverse) of addition.

questions.question: What is 3² equal to?
questions.explanation: 3² = 3 × 3 = 9. The exponent 2 means multiply the base by itself.

questions.question: Factorise: 4x + 8
questions.explanation: The HCF of 4x and 8 is 4. So 4x + 8 = 4(x + 2).

questions.question: What is the y-intercept of y = 3x + 5?
questions.explanation: In y = mx + c, c is the y-intercept. Here c = 5.

questions.question: Solve: 3x = 18
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 3: x = 18 ÷ 3 = 6.

lessons.title: Volume and Surface Area of Solids

content.body:
Cuboid: V=lwh, SA=2(lw+lh+wh)
Cube: V=s³, SA=6s²
Cylinder: V=πr²h, SA=2πr²+2πrh
Cone: V=⅓πr²h, SA=πr²+πrl (l=slant height)
Sphere: V=⁴⁄₃πr³, SA=4πr²
Pyramid: V=⅓×base area×height

content.title: Volume Problems
content.body:
Cuboid 5×4×3: V=60cm³, SA=2(20+15+12)=94cm²
Cylinder r=3, h=10: V=π×9×10=90π≈283cm³
Sphere r=4: V=⁴⁄₃π×64=268cm³
Cone r=3, h=4, l=5: V=⅓π×9×4=12π≈37.7cm³

content.title: Practical Problems
content.body:
A can (cylinder) has diameter 8cm, height 15cm.
r=4, V=π×16×15=240π≈754cm³
SA=2π×16+2π×4×15=32π+120π=152π≈477cm²

questions.question: A water tank cylinder r=1.5m, h=2m. Volume (π=3.14)
Volume of pyramid base area 20, height 9
Curved surface area of cylinder r=3, h=8 (π=3.14)

content.body:
Similar shapes: same shape, different size
All corresponding angles equal
Corresponding sides in same ratio (scale factor k)

If scale factor = k:
Length ratio = k
Area ratio = k²
Volume ratio = k³

content.title: Similar Triangles
content.body:
Triangles ABC and DEF are similar. AB=6, DE=9.
Scale factor k = 9/6 = 1.5
If BC=8, EF=8×1.5=12
If area ABC=24cm², area DEF=24×1.5²=24×2.25=54cm²

content.title: Scale Drawing
content.body:
Map scale 1:50000 means 1cm on map = 50000cm = 500m real
5cm on map = 5×500=2500m=2.5km real
Real distance 3km = 3000m = 300000cm → map: 300000/50000=6cm

questions.question: Scale factor between similar triangles is 3. If small side=5, large side=
Area ratio when scale factor=2
questions.explanation: Area ratio = k² = 2²=4

questions.question: Volume ratio when scale factor=3
questions.explanation: Volume ratio=k³=3³=27

questions.question: Triangles similar, sides 4 and 10. Angle in larger corresponding to 40° in smaller
questions.explanation: Similar triangles have equal corresponding angles

questions.question: If two similar solids have surface area ratio 9:25, volume ratio is
questions.explanation: If area ratio=k², k=3:5; volume ratio=k³=27:125

questions.question: Similar rectangles: small is 3×5, large is 6×?
questions.explanation: Scale factor=2, so ?=5×2=10

questions.question: Two similar cylinders have radii 2 and 6. Volume ratio is
questions.explanation: k=2:6=1:3, volume ratio=1:27

questions.question: Scale factor 4. Large area is 80cm². Small area =
questions.explanation: Area ratio=16, small=80/16=5cm²

content.body: In this lesson, we explore advanced aspects of Mensuration. Build on what you have already learned and apply your knowledge to more challenging problems.
Learning Objectives

By the end of this lesson you should be able to: (1) Apply Mensuration concepts to complex problems. (2) Use appropriate formulas and methods. (3) Interpret results in context. (4) Connect this topic to related areas of mathematics.
Core Theory

This section of Mensuration builds on foundational knowledge. In mathematics, advanced problem-solving requires not just knowing formulas but understanding when and how to apply them. Study each worked example carefully.

content.title: Worked Example 4.1
content.body: Consider a more complex problem on Mensuration. Approach: (1) Read and understand the problem. (2) Identify all given information. (3) Determine which concepts apply. (4) Solve systematically, showing all steps. (5) Check your answer is reasonable.

content.title: Worked Example 4.2
content.body: Another type of problem you may encounter: Questions may test your ability to combine multiple concepts within Mensuration. Practice identifying the type of problem and the appropriate method before attempting to solve.
Common examination questions on Mensuration include: definitions and explanations, numerical calculations, diagram interpretation, and analysis of given data. Make sure you can handle all types.
Summary and Review

You have now covered all lessons in Mensuration. Review the key concepts from each lesson, practice past paper questions, and ensure you can apply your knowledge confidently. Good luck in your studies!

questions.question: What does simplifying an expression mean?
options: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.answer: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.explanation: Simplification means rewriting an expression in its simplest equivalent form by collecting like terms and applying arithmetic.

questions.question: Which of these is an example of a variable?
questions.explanation: A variable is a letter (like x, y, n) that represents an unknown or changing value.

questions.question: What is 2x + 3x simplified?
questions.explanation: Collect like terms: 2x + 3x = 5x (both terms have the same variable x).

questions.question: Solve for x: x + 7 = 15
questions.explanation: Subtract 7 from both sides: x = 15 - 7 = 8.

questions.question: What is the result of expanding 4(y - 3)?
questions.explanation: Distribute: 4 × y - 4 × 3 = 4y - 12.

questions.question: Which operation reverses addition?
questions.explanation: Subtraction is the inverse (reverse) of addition.

questions.question: What is 3² equal to?
questions.explanation: 3² = 3 × 3 = 9. The exponent 2 means multiply the base by itself.

questions.question: Factorise: 4x + 8
questions.explanation: The HCF of 4x and 8 is 4. So 4x + 8 = 4(x + 2).

questions.question: What is the y-intercept of y = 3x + 5?
questions.explanation: In y = mx + c, c is the y-intercept. Here c = 5.

questions.question: Solve: 3x = 18
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 3: x = 18 ÷ 3 = 6.

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a rectangle?
questions.explanation: Area of a rectangle = length × width.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a like term with 3x²?
questions.explanation: Like terms must have the same variable AND the same power. 3x has power 1, not 2, so it is not a like term with 3x².

questions.question: What is the gradient/slope of a horizontal line?
questions.explanation: A horizontal line has no rise, so gradient = rise/run = 0/run = 0.

questions.question: If a = 4 and b = 3, find 2a + b.
questions.explanation: Substitute: 2(4) + 3 = 8 + 3 = 11.

questions.question: What does the inequality x > 5 mean?
questions.explanation: The > symbol means 'greater than'. x > 5 means x takes values greater than (but not equal to) 5.

questions.question: Solve: 2(x + 3) = 14
questions.explanation: Expand: 2x + 6 = 14. Subtract 6: 2x = 8. Divide: x = 4.

questions.question: The HCF of 12 and 18 is:
questions.explanation: Factors of 12: 1,2,3,4,6,12. Factors of 18: 1,2,3,6,9,18. Highest common factor = 6.

questions.question: What is the LCM of 4 and 6?
questions.explanation: Multiples of 4: 4,8,12,... Multiples of 6: 6,12,... Lowest common multiple = 12.

questions.question: Which number is prime?
questions.explanation: A prime number has exactly two factors: 1 and itself. 7 is prime (factors: 1 and 7 only).
